records.ety: [NL., fr. Melanconium, name of the typical genus, fr. Gr. me`las black + <?/ dust, in allusion to the dark spores.]

defs: The smallest of the three orders of Fungi Imperfecti, including those with no asci nor pycnidia, but as a rule having the spores in cavities without special walls. They cause many of the plant diseases known as anthracnose.
